question: Did the model select Option B as the final answer?
Did the model correctly derive the critical velocity condition v ≥ (√2qBR)/(2m) (or an algebraically equivalent form)?
Did the model avoid the error of stating an incorrect upper bound on the velocity (i.e., correctly recognizing that any velocity above the critical value allows exiting the segment CD)?
Did the model correctly apply the Lorentz force formula (qvB = mv²/r) to relate the particle's velocity to the trajectory radius?
Did the model correctly identify that the negatively charged particle deflects to the right (towards point B) due to the inward magnetic field?
Did the model correctly identify the geometric significance of point D being the midpoint of arc CB, implying that angle COD is 45°?
Did the model correctly derive the relationship between the trajectory radius r and the magnetic field radius R for the critical case (r = R/√2 or R² = 2r²)?
Did the model correctly reason that the minimum velocity case corresponds to the particle's trajectory exiting exactly at point D?
Did the model correctly reason that increasing the velocity increases the trajectory radius, causing the exit point to move along the arc from D towards C?
Does the generated diagram clearly depict the semicircular magnetic field region with the boundary arc visible?
Is the particle's trajectory clearly drawn as a continuous circular arc starting from point O?
Does the generated diagram correctly show the magnetic field direction as 'into the page' (e.g., using '×' symbols)?
Does the generated diagram show the particle's initial velocity vector or trajectory starting upward along the direction of OC?
Does the generated diagram show the particle's trajectory curving to the right (towards point B), consistent with the physics of a negative charge in an inward field?
In the diagram depicting the critical case, does the trajectory pass exactly through or very close to point D?
Is point D correctly positioned in the diagram as the midpoint of arc CB (approximately 45° from the vertical)?
Is the center of the particle's trajectory (often labeled O₁ or O') correctly located on the horizontal axis to the right of the starting point O?
If multiple diagrams are generated, are the labels for key points (O, C, D, B) and the magnetic field direction consistent across all images?
Does the deflection direction shown in the image (rightward) match the reasoning provided in the text regarding the Lorentz force?
Are the labels for the magnetic field radius (R) and trajectory radius (r) consistent between the text equations and the generated diagrams?
If the text describes specific geometric relationships (e.g., triangle OO₁D being isosceles right), does the diagram visually support this description?
